_metadata.description: Hattie's 3-Phase Learning Model — Surface → Deep → Transfer. Canvas audit indicators per phase, hattie_phase audit-tag values, BYUI module-element mapping.
_metadata.runtime_strategy_rationale: Body MD ~6,396 chars / 4 ≈ 1,599 tokens — within the 'embed' band per runtime_strategy_rules. Override to read_at_runtime per named_override_patterns.selective_load: canvas_course_expert loads 2-3 of ~12 knowledge files per audit task. Selective-load defeats embed-cache benefits.

scope: Three-phase learning progression model — Surface, Deep, Transfer — used by canvas_course_expert to tag audit issues with their hattie_phase. Boundaries: does not cover working-memory mechanics within a phase (see cognitive_load_theory) or brain-aligned sequencing (see experiential_learning).

facts.definition: Phase 1 (Surface) → Phase 2 (Deep) → Phase 3 (Transfer). A well-designed course scaffolds all three in sequence. A gap in an earlier phase blocks progression to the next. A course cannot support Deep if Surface is broken; cannot support Transfer if Deep is absent.

Students acquire foundational knowledge — the 'what' and 'how'. They need clear navigation, sequenced content, and low extraneous load so they can focus on new information rather than figuring out where to go.

Module has overview page (outcomes, time estimate, sequence); content items clearly labeled and logically ordered; predictable navigation with consistent module naming, no orphaned pages; item count ≤7 per module; all items in a published module are also published.
citations: This knowledge file's Surface audit table

facts.definition: No overview page; module starts with assessment; inconsistent module naming; unpublished items in live module; >7 items in module; duplicate instructions. Surface gaps are almost always extraneous load.
citations: This knowledge file's Surface audit table

facts.definition: Students connect ideas, find patterns, and build understanding — the 'why' and 'when'. They need activities that require them to explain, compare, debate, or teach. Surface knowledge is raw material; Deep is where it becomes understanding.

Discussion activities where students apply or explain concepts (BYUI: Teach One Another); assignments requiring synthesis, comparison, or judgment; learning outcomes explicitly stated on overview pages; peer review or collaborative tasks; reflection prompts tied to content.
citations: This knowledge file's Deep audit section

facts.definition: Module has only quizzes and readings; all assessments are multiple-choice (recall, not understanding); no discussion or peer interaction; overview page exists but states no outcomes. Deep gaps often mean germane load is absent — no activity is doing schema-building work.
citations: This knowledge file's Deep audit table

facts.definition: Students apply knowledge to new, unfamiliar contexts — the 'what if' and 'so what'. Transfer is the goal of education. It requires authentic tasks with real-world stakes or cross-domain application, where students make judgment calls rather than follow a procedure.

Project-based or milestone assessments with open-ended scope; self-assessment or reflection assignments; assignments requiring application to problems the course hasn't explicitly modeled; capstone tasks integrating multiple modules; students make design/judgment decisions (not just execute known procedures).
citations: This knowledge file's Transfer audit section

facts.definition: No project or milestone assignment; all assessments are closed (structured lab with answer key, quiz); no self-assessment or end-of-unit reflection; module ends immediately after a quiz. Transfer gaps are usually symptoms of CLT failures earlier in the sequence.
citations: This knowledge file's Transfer audit table

facts.definition: Every audit issue includes a hattie_phase field with value: surface (issue blocks foundational acquisition), deep (issue prevents connection and understanding), transfer (issue removes authentic application), or all (issue impairs entire learning progression). Prioritize fixing 'all' first, then surface, deep, transfer.
citations: This knowledge file's Phase Gap Tags section

facts.definition: Overview page → Surface (orients, reduces extraneous load). Content (readings, videos, demos) → Surface (foundational acquisition). NotebookLM/AI tools → Surface→Deep (acquisition + elaboration). Teach One Another (discussion) → Deep (peer construction of meaning). Stand Up Report/quiz → Deep→Transfer. Prove It (assignment, milestone) → Transfer. Self-assessment/reflection → Transfer (metacognitive consolidation).
citations: BYUI module structure; this knowledge file's mapping table

sources: Hattie, J. (2009). Visible Learning: A Synthesis of Over 800 Meta-Analyses Relating to Achievement. Routledge.

pre_run_checklist: Each entry in consumed_by[] resolves to an existing agent spec
post_run_checklist: knowledge/hattie_3phase_knowledge.json exists with shape=reference and facts[] present

changes: Retrofit: JSON companion authored for pre-existing canvas-toolbox knowledge MD.
MD body and canvas-toolbox header convention preserved.
Runtime strategy set to read_at_runtime via selective_load override (embed band by size; selective audit-task loading pattern).
